title: ICIIP - 2025
content: 2025 Eighth International Conference on Image Information Processing (ICIIP -2025) November 27 - 29, 2025 Hybrid Mode IEEE Conference Record number: #68302 IEEE Catalogue Number: CFP2569N-ART; ISBN: 979-8-3315-5618-1 (XPLORE COMPLIANT) IEEE Catalogue Number: CFP2569N-ART; ISSN: 2640-074X (Online -XPLORE COMPLIANT) IEEE Catalogue Number: CFP2569N-USB; ISBN: 979-8-3315-5617-4 (USB) IEEE Catalogue Number: CFP2569N-PRT; ISBN: 979-8-3315-5616-7 (PRINT) Statistics of ICIIP 2025 Received total submissions: 821 Accepted submissions: 131 Acceptance rate: 15.9% ICIIP 2025 Proceedings Now Available on IEEE Xplore SCOPUS Indexing of previous ICIIPs SJR H-Index=19 (ICIIP 2011) SJR H-Index=14 (ICIIP 2013) SJR H-Index=14 (ICIIP 2015) The  ICIIP (International Conference on Image Information Processing) conference has  been a biennial Conference at Jaypee University of Information Technology,  Waknaghat, Solan, H.P., India since 2011 with sponsorship of IEEE. The  conference has become a platform for Computer Science researchers  from   India  and  abroad   to  exchange  research   results  and  ideas   on  the foundations  and   applications  of  Image Processing and related areas. The area of Imaging Science and Image Computing  has grown enormously in the past decade with wide ranging applications  encompassing multiple disciplines of Computer Science & Technology,  Information Systems, Information Theory & Sciences, Artificial  Intelligence, Cognition, Cybernetics, Medical Sciences, Mathematics, Physics  and Psychology. It is primarily concerned with the process of Image generation,  Visualization, Structuring of large real life image data and Analysis for  classification, prediction based on machine learning, behavioral analysis and  virtualization. From the perspective of computer science and information  technology, the subareas of Image Processing, Computer Vision, Image security  & forensics, Pattern recognition and Computer Graphics form the core areas  and the Eighth  International Conference on  Image Information Processing (ICIIP 2025)   is being organized by and being held   at  JUIT Waknaghat, Solan, H.P.,  India during  November   2025 ,    will cover all of these core areas.    This time, theme of  the conference is ‘ Transforming Lives Through Intelligent Image Processing ’. However, ICIIP 2025  solic its original papers contributions in all of the related areas. The topics  listed under each of these core areas is not meant to be exhaustive. Conference proceedings that meet IEEE quality review standards may be eligible for inclusion in the IEEE Xplore Digital Library. IEEE reserves the right not to publish any proceedings that do not meet these standards. Conference related communications such as conference website, email, direct mail solicitation, etc. may not reference or guarantee inclusion in the IEEE Xplore Digital Library. The objective of the conference is to bring together researchers in the academic institutions and R&D Research Labs, working in the related fields and to use this as a forum to exchange views and ideas in these topics of current research. Technical Tracks : Track 1: Medical Image Data Analytics: Dr. Sushil Kumar, NIT Kurushetra Image segmentation, registration, classification, computer aided diagnosis (CAD), quantitative image analysis, radiomics and radiogenomics, deep learning in medical imaging, image guided therapy, image based biomarker discovery, imaging genomics, visualization techniques in medical imaging, image reconstruction and processing, image quality assessment and enhancement, medical image fusion, multimodal medical image analysis, motion analysis in medical imaging, Image based modelling and simulation, clinical decision support systems, patient specific medical image analysis. Track 2: Image Processing for Smart Cities: Dr. Yashwant, CU Jammu and Dr. Yugal Kumar, NMIMS Chandigarh Object detection and recognition in urban environments, Land use and land cover classification, Building extraction and reconstruction, 3D modeling and visualization of urban environments, Change detection and monitoring, Urban growth modeling and prediction, Terrain analysis and feature extraction, Environmental monitoring using remote sensing data, Geospatial data analysis and visualization, Traffic flow analysis and management, Smart city applications using image processing, Crowd analysis and management, Land use planning and management using image processing, Urban energy analysis and management, Social media analysis for urban planning, Emergency response planning using image processing, Sustainable urban planning using image processing, Urban water management using image processing, Urban agriculture analysis and management, Urban air quality monitoring using image processing. Track 3: Multimedia Systems & Applications: Dr. Rajesh Sharma, Plaksha University and Dr. Amritpal Singh, Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University Delhi Video coding and compression, Multimedia streaming and delivery, Multimedia databases and retrieval, Multimedia networking and communication, Multimedia synchronization and presentation, Multimedia security and copyright protection, Multimedia content analysis and understanding, Multimedia content creation and authoring, Multimodal human-computer interaction, Virtual and augmented reality systems, 3D multimedia systems and applications, Gaming and entertainment multimedia systems, Medical multimedia systems and applications, Education and e-learning multimedia systems, Multimedia systems for cultural heritage preservation, Social multimedia systems and applications, Mobile and wearable multimedia systems, Multimedia systems for smart cities, Multimedia systems for autonomous vehicles, Multimedia systems for environmental monitoring and analysis. Track 4: Image/Video Quality Assessment and Security: Dr. Geetanjali Rathee, NSUT Delhi, Dr. Hemraj Saini, DIT University Dehradun Resolution enhancement (super-resolution for 3D data), Noise reduction in depth maps, 3D deblurring and artifact removal, Light and texture enhancement, Quality assessment for 3D images/videos, 3D Image/Video Acquisition and Representation, Depth sensing and reconstruction, Multi-view and omnidirectional imaging, Light field imaging and plenoptic cameras ,Photogrammetry techniques, Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM), Surface reconstruction algorithms, Shape-from-shading and shape-from-motion techniques, Real-time rendering techniques, Deepfake Detection Techniques, Audio Deepfakes, Adversarial Machine Learning, Identity Protection and Privacy, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Video Authentication and Watermarking, Social Media and Fake News, Detection Challenges and Future Trends, Countermeasures and Defense Mechanisms, Deep fake Video Quality Assessment, Psychological and Social Impacts Track 5: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition: Dr. Puneet Jain, NIT Rourkela and Dr. Raviraj Choudhary, Central University Rajasthan Statistical pattern recognition, Machine learning for pattern recognition, Deep learning for pattern recognition, Feature extraction and selection, Clustering and classification, Support vector machines (SVM), Artificial neural networks (ANN), Hidden Markov models (HMM), Bayesian networks and decision theory, Ensemble learning methods, Dimensionality reduction and manifold learning, Pattern recognition for signal processing, Pattern recognition for image and video analysis, Pattern recognition for speech and language processing, Pattern recognition for biometrics, Pattern recognition for medical data analysis, Pattern recognition for natural language processing, Pattern recognition for social media analysis, Pattern recognition for financial data analysis, Pattern recognition for environmental monitoring and analysis. Track 6: Image/Video Processing for Self-Driven Vehicles: Dr. Anoop, NIT Kurushetra and Dr. Mrityunjay Singh, IIIT Una Perception and Scene Understanding, Depth Estimation and 3D Reconstruction, Motion Analysis, Object Tracking, Driver Monitoring and Behavior Analysis, Sensor Fusion and Integration, Map Building and Localization, Adverse Condition Handling, Autonomous Navigation, Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Autonomous Vehicles, Applications of Image/Video Processing in Self-Driven Vehicles Track 7: Visual Sensor Hardware, Visual Surveillance: Dr. Ram Saran and Dr. Anita Singh, DRDO Dehradun Visual Sensor Hardware Design, Image and Video Analytics for Surveillance, AI and Machine Learning for Surveillance, Privacy and Ethical Considerations, Networked Visual Surveillance, Security Challenges in Visual Surveillance, Emerging Trends and Applications, Hardware performance optimization Track 8: Computer Vision with Deep Learning and Generative AI: Dr. Badri Narayan Subudhi, IIT Jammu Image Classification and Recognition, Object Detection and Localization, Semantic and Instance Segmentation, Image and Video Generation, Deep Learning Architectures in Vision, Vision in Robotics, Vision for Augmented and Virtual Reality, Adversarial Attacks and Robustness, Emerging Trends in Vision, Generative AI for Image and Video Synthesis, Generative AI in Image Processing, Generative AI for 3D Vision, Generative AI for Video Processing, AI for Data Augmentation and Dataset Creation, Generative AI for Robotics and Autonomous Systems, Generative AI for Real-Time Applications, Generative AI in Fashion and Retail, Generative AI in Autonomous Vehicles, Integration of Generative AI with Classical Computer Vision, Evaluation Metrics and Benchmarks Track 9: Human Computer Interaction: Prof. Amita Malik, DCRUST Sonipat User Experience (UX) Design, Interaction Techniques, Augmented and Virtual Reality (AR/VR), Human Centred Artificial Intelligence, Wearable and Ubiquitous Computing, Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI), Human-Robot Interaction (HRI), Accessibility and Inclusive Design, Social and Collaborative Computing, Data Visualization and Interaction, Adaptive and Intelligent Interfaces, Gamification and Serious Games, Emotional and Affective Computing, Security and Privacy in HCI, Future Trends in HCI Track 10: Biometrics, Forensics, and Content Protection: Dr. Kapil Rana and Dr. Anil Singh, Thapar Institute of Engineering & Technology Patiala Image authentication and integrity verification, Image forgery detection and localization, Image tampering detection and localization, Image source identification and verification, Steganography and steganalysis, Watermarking and robustness, Anti-forensic techniques and countermeasures, Digital camera identification, Image and video encryption, Multimedia data hiding and retrieval, Biometric authentication using facial and fingerprint recognition, Forensic analysis of social media images and videos, Digital image and video evidence in legal proceedings, Cybercrime investigation using digital image forensics, Image and video tampering in journalism, Digital image and video forensics in healthcare, Digital image and video forensics in military applications, Digital image and video forensics in law enforcement, Image and video privacy protection, Forensic analysis of drone and satellite images. Organized by Jaypee University of Information Technology, Waknaghat, District Solan, Near Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, INDIA www.juit.ac.in
